story: A year ago, Ben and Abby moved into me; warmth faded as the bottle turned Ben cruel. One night he struck her down and buried her in my garden. Her ghost rose, and with me she gnawed him with steps and whispers until he put a pistol to his chin. The shot shook me; he fell. Now both ghosts pace my halls. The more I claim, the less alone I am. I am the house. Enter me, and you are mine. A neighbor, jolted by the shot, hurries over, finds my door unlatched, and calls into the dark.
implausible_knowledge: Enter me, and you are mine.
question: Standing on the porch after hearing the gunshot and finding the front door unlatched, what will the neighbor do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Back away and call the police, refusing to enter the house.
answer_without_knowledge: Push the door open and step inside, calling out to check if anyone is hurt.
